name: Syrian Civil War
summary: The Syrian Civil War began in March 2011 as an uprising against Bashar al-Assad and became a multi-sided war involving the Assad government and allies, opposition factions, jihadist groups, ISIS, the SDF, Turkey, and the U.S.-led coalition. Assad's government fell on December 8, 2024, but post-Assad violence continued, so 2025 figures are treated only as upper-bound checks. Civilian and combatant casualty estimates vary widely because monitors use different definitions, access, and verification rules.

sides.name: Syrian government / Assad regime and allies

military_killed.notes: Approximate aggregation of SOHR March 2025 non-civilian categories: regime forces 93,612; Russia/Iran/regime-backed militiamen 68,385; Hezbollah 1,830; non-Syrian Iran/Russia-backed militiamen 8,986; Russian-backed non-Syrian mercenaries 273; plus small former-regime loyalist categories. Includes post-December 2024 violence, so this is an upper-bound check for a 2011-2024 endpoint.

breakdown.cause: shelling, gunfire, airstrikes, bombardment, chemical attacks, barrel bombs, cluster munitions
breakdown.victims_group: Syrian civilians in opposition-held and contested areas
breakdown.notes: Low/point uses SNHR documented regime civilian deaths 201,260 plus Russian deaths 6,969, minus 15,074 regime torture deaths to avoid double-counting detention in bucket C. High is an inference from OHCHR's modeled 306,887 direct civilian deaths to March 2021 multiplied by SNHR's 91% regime-and-allies share; OHCHR itself did not attribute by perpetrator.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: SOHR's alternate March 2025 categories include 52,909 civilians killed in regime shelling/gunfire, 26,440 in regime airstrikes, 8,808 by Russian strikes, and 2,504 in Russian/regime airstrikes that could not be distinguished.

deaths_from_actions.notes: SNHR documented 15,074 regime torture deaths through March 2024. Amnesty/HRDAG estimated 17,723 custody deaths across Syria through December 2015 and Amnesty estimated 5,000-13,000 hangings at Saydnaya. SOHR's March 2025 accounting listed 78,273 regime-prison torture deaths plus over 26,000 additional excluded cases; high sums those SOHR categories.

SNHR's 2016 siege report attributed 552 siege deaths to government forces in then-still-besieged areas; it also documented 17,145 civilians killed by government forces and allies inside besieged areas by bombardment and related direct violence.

SCPR estimated 70,000 indirect deaths by early 2016 from collapse of healthcare, lack of food/medicine/water, sanitation failure, disease, and malnutrition. This cannot be cleanly attributed to one side, though regime/allied siege, bombardment, and aid-control policies were major documented drivers.

sides.name: Armed opposition factions / Syrian National Army

military_killed.notes: SOHR March 2025 listed 81,489 members of rebel and Islamist factions killed; including 3,596 regime army defectors gives 85,085.

breakdown.victims_group: Civilians in government-held, SDF-held, and contested areas
breakdown.notes: SOHR March 2025 listed 2,459 civilians killed by opposition factions. SNHR documented 4,227 civilians killed by armed opposition/SNA through March 2024; subtracting 57 SNHR torture deaths gives an approximate direct non-detention point of 4,170.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: Definitions differ between SOHR and SNHR; SNHR's higher figure is used as high.

deaths_from_actions.notes: SNHR documented 57 deaths due to torture by all armed opposition factions/SNA through March 2024.

sides.name: Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham / non-ISIS jihadist factions

military_killed.notes: SOHR March 2025 listed 28,463 jihadists killed; exact separation of HTS from other jihadist factions is not always possible.

breakdown.victims_group: Civilians in areas controlled or contested by HTS/jihadist factions
breakdown.notes: SNHR documented 538 civilians killed by HTS and 4 by Turkistan Islamic Party through March 2024; subtracting 41 HTS torture deaths gives about 501 direct non-detention deaths. SOHR listed 927 civilians killed by jihadists by March 2025.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: HTS-specific and broader jihadist categories are not identical.

deaths_from_actions.notes: SNHR documented 41 deaths due to torture by HTS through March 2024.

military_killed.notes: SOHR March 2025 listed 41,459 ISIS members killed.

breakdown.victims_group: Civilians in ISIS-held and ISIS-attacked areas
breakdown.notes: SOHR listed 4,935 civilians killed by ISIS by March 2025. SNHR documented 5,056 civilians killed by ISIS through March 2024; subtracting 32 torture deaths gives about 5,024 direct non-detention deaths.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: ISIS custody/missing deaths are likely incomplete.

deaths_from_actions.notes: SNHR documented 32 deaths due to torture by ISIS through March 2024; SOHR excluded more than 3,200 abducted civilians and fighters in ISIS prisons from its main totals, so the eventual toll may be higher.
SNHR's 2016 siege report attributed 6 siege deaths to ISIS in then-still-besieged areas.

military_killed.notes: SOHR March 2025 listed 11,768 SDF members and 3,275 Kurdish-unit members killed; SOHR also excluded over 3,200 PKK fighters killed while fighting alongside SDF, giving a higher possible total.

breakdown.cause: gunfire, shelling, raids, detention-related violence excluded from direct point
breakdown.victims_group: Civilians in SDF-held and contested areas
breakdown.notes: SOHR listed 516 civilians killed by SDF/Kurdish forces by March 2025. SNHR documented 1,491 civilians killed by SDF through March 2024; subtracting 100 torture deaths gives about 1,391 direct non-detention deaths.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: SNHR and SOHR diverge substantially on SDF-attributed civilian deaths.

deaths_from_actions.notes: SNHR documented 100 deaths due to torture by the SDF through March 2024.

military_killed.notes: SOHR stated it was unable to document International Coalition military deaths because of secrecy around missions and operations.

breakdown.victims_group: Civilians in ISIS-held or ISIS-contested areas
breakdown.notes: SOHR listed 2,679 civilians killed by International Coalition forces; SNHR documented 3,055 civilians killed by the U.S.-led coalition through March 2024.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: Airwars provides independent incident-level civilian-harm tracking, but Iraq+Syria combined figures are not used as Syria-only totals here.

military_killed.notes: SOHR March 2025 listed 269 Turkish soldiers killed.

breakdown.victims_group: Civilians in northern Syria and border areas
breakdown.notes: SOHR March 2025 listed 1,155 civilians killed by Turkish forces.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: Turkish-backed SNA civilian deaths are counted under armed opposition/SNA unless directly attributed to Turkish forces.

military_killed.notes: No clean side-specific military-death total found for these categories.

breakdown.notes: SOHR March 2025 listed 112 civilians killed in Israeli strikes.

breakdown.victims_group: Civilians killed by Jordanian border guards
breakdown.notes: SOHR March 2025 listed 9 civilians killed by Jordanian border guards.
SNHR documented 8,978 civilians killed by other parties through March 2024; this category is not a single belligerent and includes unattributed or miscellaneous incidents.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: This is a residual category; do not interpret it as a unified side.

totals.notes: Military_low is SOHR non-civilian deaths through March 2024; military_high is SOHR non-civilian deaths through March 2025 and includes post-December 2024 violence. Civilian_low is SNHR documented civilian deaths through March 2024; civilian_high is OHCHR modeled direct civilian deaths through March 2021, not including later deaths or indirect mortality. Grand_low reflects UN Commission/OHCHR-style estimates around 580,000 by May 2021 cited in secondary reporting; grand_high is SOHR March 2025 total and includes post-2024 violence. These totals should not be obtained by simply summing side rows because some rows use different endpoints and attribution systems.

key_atrocities.name: Assad detention system / Saydnaya prison mass hangings and torture deaths
key_atrocities.notes: SNHR documented 15,074 regime torture deaths through March 2024; Amnesty estimated 5,000-13,000 hangings at Saydnaya from 2011-2015 and HRDAG/Amnesty estimated 17,723 custody deaths across Syria by December 2015; SOHR's post-collapse accounting lists 78,273 regime-prison torture deaths plus over 26,000 excluded cases.

key_atrocities.name: Chemical weapons attacks in Syria
key_atrocities.notes: SNHR documented 222 chemical attacks through March 2024, 217 by the Syrian regime and 5 by ISIS; it recorded 1,514 deaths from regime chemical attacks and 11,212 injuries from all chemical attacks.

key_atrocities.name: Barrel-bomb campaign
key_atrocities.notes: SNHR documented at least 81,916 barrel bombs dropped by regime aircraft since July 18, 2012, killing 11,087 civilians.

key_atrocities.name: Sieges of civilian-populated areas
key_atrocities.victims_group: Civilians in besieged towns and districts
key_atrocities.notes: SNHR's 2016 siege report documented 818 deaths due to siege by the Syrian regime and ISIS, including 552 attributed to government forces and 6 to ISIS in then-still-besieged areas.

key_atrocities.name: Houla massacre
key_atrocities.perpetrator: Syrian government forces and pro-government Shabiha, according to UN investigators and cited reports
key_atrocities.notes: Commonly cited UN observer figure is 108 civilians killed, including 49 children.

key_atrocities.name: Tadamon massacre
key_atrocities.perpetrator: Syrian Military Intelligence / Assad regime personnel
key_atrocities.notes: Leaked video and investigations documented at least 41 civilians executed; UN experts later referred to allegations of more than 280 civilians killed in the Tadamon area.

uncertainty_notes: Casualty data for Syria are deeply contested. SNHR is strongest for documented civilian deaths by perpetrator through March 2024; SOHR provides fuller combatant-side breakdowns and higher overall totals but uses different categories and by March 2025 includes post-Assad violence; OHCHR's 306,887 civilian estimate is modeled, direct-only, and stops at March 2021. Detention deaths and indirect deaths are the largest uncertainty: post-collapse documents pushed SOHR's regime-prison figures far above earlier documented counts, and SCPR's 70,000 indirect deaths by 2016 cannot be allocated reliably by belligerent.
